metadata.description: Per-spot physical parameters for synthetic label formula and model evaluation.
metadata.references: Surfline spot guides and mechanics articles; see tracker for per-spot sources.

preferred_tide_band.notes: Surfline does not specify metre range; window is from peak low to peak high.

pipeline.wind_ceiling_notes: Highly exposed reef; needs very light winds to stay clean. E to S winds preferred.

preferred_tide_band.notes: Surfline explicitly lists 'all tides' as best; no narrower window.

hossegor.wind_ceiling_notes: Exposed beachbreak; best with calm to light/moderate offshore (easterly) wind.

preferred_tide_band.notes: Tide preference varies by spot within Ericeira zone; Ribeira d'Ilhas prefers mid, Pedra Branca prefers mid-to-high.

ericeira.wind_ceiling_notes: Exposed Atlantic reef/point zone; clean surf needs light offshore flow (E winds preferred).
ericeira.source: Surfline Ericeira surf report and guide context

preferred_tide_band.notes: Tide preference depends on swell direction and strength; some swells best on low, others on high. Mid-incoming tide often works well.

jeffreys_bay.wind_ceiling_notes: Open, high-quality point system; lighter offshore winds (SW to W) needed for clean lines.
jeffreys_bay.source: Surfline Jeffreys Bay surf guide and mechanics article

swell_direction_range.description: Not explicitly stated in Surfline surfaced content; Gold Coast is a multi-break zone with varying aspects (55°–200° estimated from geography, but requires field confirmation).

preferred_tide_band.notes: Gold Coast contains multiple breaks with different tide preferences; no unified band available in Surfline surfaced pages. Some pointbreak/breakwall spots prefer high tide.

gold_coast.wind_ceiling_notes: Mixed beachbreak/pointbreak region; cleaner surf generally comes with lighter winds. Local variation high.
gold_coast.source: Surfline Gold Coast travel zone pages and report (limited detail in surfaced content)

notes.incomplete_data: Gold Coast swell direction and tide band not explicitly found in Surfline surfaced pages. Recommend: (1) check Surfline direct for Gold Coast mechanics, or (2) use typical NE to SE swell for eastern Australia coast breaks and research tide at target spots individually.
notes.synthetic_label_usage: These parameters ground the synthetic label formula in Section 2.2 of the thesis. Swell direction and tide band inform the offshore-wind-alignment and tidal-falloff terms; wind ceiling caps the rideable-conditions term.
notes.reproducibility: Each parameter is sourced and dated. A reviewer can cross-check swell direction against Surfline or Wannasurf; tide bands are less standardized but can be verified against local hydrographic data or surf guides per spot.
